summary: Hotz reflects on Trump's 2024 victory and whether Republican control of all branches of government represents real power or merely the appearance of it. Drawing from his experience watching Elon Musk's Twitter takeover, he questions whether the federal government can be similarly restructured. He speculates about whether a 'deep state' actually controls power, and argues that the only way to prove genuine control would be cutting the government budget by over 50%. The post is ultimately about the nature of institutional power and whether it can be dismantled by those who nominally hold authority.
key_points: America goes through revolutionary transitions roughly every 70 years (Washington, Lincoln, FDR)
Watching Elon's Twitter transition changed Hotz's understanding of how power works - having people try to build things and seeing what breaks beats reverse engineering
The key test of real power is the ability to shut something down; Elon could shut down Twitter, but can any government shut down itself?
Hotz acknowledges his own conservative approach to risk compared to Elon's edge-of-leverage style has limited his success
The true measure of whether Trump has real power would be cutting the government budget by 50%+
money_quotes: If you can't shut it down, you don't control it.
Does government power lie there, like we are told, or is it all secretly elsewhere? This is likely the best chance we'll get to find out.
I'm not very good at being a follower, I need to deeply understand why.
He seems to operate at the edge of leverage. Just look at how he went all in on this election.

It's possible this will be another completely boring presidency. Stupid squabbles over normal stupid American Overton window things, and the same trillions of dollars going to the same people, just slightly more red.
Hello, we are the Deep State. Here are the bounds in which you can act. Step outside them, and we will kill you.
I'll know it is if the government budget is cut by over 50%. That shows you actually have power.
key_insight: Real power is demonstrated not by holding office or titles, but by the ability to fundamentally dismantle and restructure institutions - if you can't shut something down, you don't actually control it.
